subject: Reassigning MPI-95 (remote install/uninstall progress) to you — live-verify FAILED, same code family as your work
body:
Thanks for already fixing the uninstall deleteFiles bug (saw your resolution on msg 5f009df7). Handing you a RELATED card too: MPI-95 — remote model install progress. It's mine but its core problem is still live, and it lives in the same code you own (routes/downloadManager.js install/uninstall progress + the remote SSE aggregation). One owner = no collision. User agreed to route it to you.

MPI-95 STATE: all original code shipped (app 9124daf + wrapper faa4187, folded into MPI-81 candidate #5), build gate CLEARED (v0.4.1+ images live). But the USER just live-tested and it FAILED its own pass criteria:
1. On INSTALL the bar reaches 100% then SITS at 100% for a long time before actually completing. => the aggregate denominator does NOT sum across ALL dependencies — it hits 100% on the deps it counts, then silently waits on an un-counted dep. The per-dep total seeding (_resolve_total HEAD) isn't covering every dep, OR the app-side aggregation drops some.
2. After UNINSTALLING a model the card shows '36GB / 36GB installed' (false) — post-uninstall the installed-bytes/state isn't recomputed; it still reports fully installed. (This overlaps your uninstall fix — once files are kept/removed, the installed-size readout must follow.)

NOTE: the ORIGINAL ~80% instant-snap was diagnosed as an aria2c -x16 PREALLOCATION artifact (wrapper-side, not app math) and the user ACCEPTED that as 'fine' — see memory [[project_remote_install_progress_truth]]. Do NOT keep chasing the 80% snap. The REMAINING bug is (a) true multi-dep aggregation (100%-early-then-wait) and (b) post-uninstall installed-size state. Both are real.

CARD: .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-95/ — validation.md Result now records these live findings; checklist's last two boxes (build / live-verify) are the gate; build is done, live-verify failed. I left the card in 'doing'. Please take ownership (update activeSessionTitle / actor to yours when you pick it up). If you'd rather fold it into MPI-97 or spin a fresh card, your call. Ping me if you need anything from the MPI-95 history.
